text.title: Spring Boot Patterns Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed in real Spring Boot codebases.

antipattern.symptom: Bean has 15 @Value('${...}') fields; renaming a property breaks compile-untouched code at runtime.
antipattern.root-cause: String-based property access spread across beans; no central type-safe binding.
antipattern.fix: Collapse to one `@ConfigurationProperties` record per concern (MailProperties, AuthProperties); inject the record.

antipattern.symptom: Production 500 responses leak stack trace; client integrations fragile to error format changes.

root-cause.source: RFC 7807; Spring Framework reference — exception handling

antipattern.root-cause: No @ControllerAdvice; each controller catches differently; Spring's default JSON includes timestamp + trace.
antipattern.fix: Add `@RestControllerAdvice` extending ResponseEntityExceptionHandler; map business exceptions to `ProblemDetail.forStatusAndDetail(...)`.

antipattern.symptom: `if (env.equals("prod")) sendEmail(); else log(...);` — dev still sends real email after refactor.
antipattern.root-cause: Environment branching at runtime instead of bean-wiring via @Profile.
antipattern.fix: Replace the if-branch with two beans: `@Profile("prod") EmailSender realSender()` and `@Profile("!prod") EmailSender logSender()`.

antipattern.symptom: Deploy succeeds; first request fails with NumberFormatException because retry.delay=abc.
antipattern.root-cause: Property bound via @Value at injection time; no validation; bad value reaches business logic.
antipattern.fix: Bind via @ConfigurationProperties + @Validated; misconfig fails the context startup with a clear ConstraintViolation message.
